message:
fix: add inline explanations to SSH prompt guidance

- Explain what the file path prompt means and where keys are saved
- Explain why no passphrase is recommended for dev VPS
- Add summary note about pressing Enter three times

Continues SSH key UX improvements from previous commit.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -273,6 +273,13 @@ export default function GenerateSSHKeyPage() {

→ Just press Enter! The path is already set by our command.

+
+ What this means: The path shown (like{" "} + /Users/you/.ssh/acfs_ed25519) is where your + key files will be saved. The .ssh folder + is a standard location for SSH keys on all computers. Our command already specifies this path, so + just press Enter to confirm it. +
@@ -283,6 +290,12 @@ export default function GenerateSSHKeyPage() {

→ Press Enter without typing anything. Leave it empty.

+
+ Why no passphrase? A passphrase would add an extra password + you'd have to type every time you connect. For a development VPS that you control, this extra + security isn't necessary and would slow you down. Your private key file itself is already secure + because it never leaves your computer. +
@@ -297,7 +310,7 @@ export default function GenerateSSHKeyPage() {

- Note: Not using a passphrase is fine for this purpose. It keeps things simple. + Summary: Press Enter three times total. The command we provided handles all the important settings.
